title: ArticleLink Schema & Migration

story.i_want_to: create typed relationships between knowledge articles
story.so_that: agents can traverse the knowledge graph and discover related patterns, contradictions, and superseded knowledge

rationale: Knowledge articles rarely exist in isolation. A pattern may be derived from a review finding, a convention may contradict an older decision, and a new article may supersede an outdated one. Typed, directional links between articles create a traversable knowledge graph that agents can use to discover related context, identify contradictions before making decisions, and follow the evolution chain of superseded knowledge. The immutable design (insert-only, no updated_at) ensures the link history is preserved.

acceptance_criteria.description: A Loopctl.Knowledge.ArticleLink schema exists with fields: id (binary_id PK), tenant_id (binary_id FK to tenants), source_article_id (binary_id FK to articles), target_article_id (binary_id FK to articles), relationship_type (Ecto.Enum: :relates_to, :derived_from, :contradicts, :supersedes), metadata (map/jsonb, default %{}), inserted_at (utc_datetime_usec). No updated_at field -- article links are immutable.
The schema uses `use Loopctl.Schema` and `tenant_field()` macro. The tenant_id field is NEVER included in cast.
A changeset validates required fields: source_article_id, target_article_id, relationship_type. It validates that source_article_id != target_article_id (no self-links).
Migration creates the article_links table with all specified columns and correct foreign key references to articles.
Migration adds a unique composite index on (tenant_id, source_article_id, target_article_id, relationship_type) preventing duplicate links of the same type between the same pair of articles.
Migration enables RLS on the article_links table via `import Loopctl.Repo.RlsHelpers` then `enable_rls(:article_links)`. RLS policies enforce tenant isolation.

The relationship_type Ecto.Enum values represent the four core link types: :relates_to (general association), :derived_from (source was created based on target), :contradicts (source conflicts with target), :supersedes (source replaces target).
The timestamps override only includes :inserted_at (no :updated_at) since article links are immutable after creation.
Foreign key constraints on source_article_id and target_article_id reference the articles table with ON DELETE CASCADE, so deleting an article removes its links.

test_cases.name: Create article link with valid data
preconditions: Loopctl.Knowledge.ArticleLink module is compiled and available
steps: Build a changeset with valid source_article_id, target_article_id, and relationship_type :relates_to
expected_results: Changeset is valid with no errors

test_cases.name: Reject self-link where source equals target
preconditions: Loopctl.Knowledge.ArticleLink module is compiled and available
steps: Build a changeset where source_article_id and target_article_id are the same UUID
expected_results: Error indicates self-links are not allowed

test_cases.name: Reject duplicate link of same type between same articles
preconditions: fixture(:article_link, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, source_article_id: article_a.id, target_article_id: article_b.id, relationship_type: :relates_to})
steps: Attempt to insert a second link with the same source, target, and relationship_type
expected_results: Insert fails with unique constraint error on the composite index

test_cases.name: Allow different relationship types between same articles
preconditions: fixture(:article_link, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, source_article_id: article_a.id, target_article_id: article_b.id, relationship_type: :relates_to})
steps: Insert a link with relationship_type :derived_from between the same articles
expected_results: Insert succeeds -- different relationship_type makes the link unique

test_cases.name: Reject missing required fields
preconditions: Loopctl.Knowledge.ArticleLink module is compiled and available
steps: Build a changeset with empty attrs (no source_article_id, target_article_id, or relationship_type)

test_cases.name: Tenant isolation via RLS prevents cross-tenant link access
preconditions: fixture(:article_link, %{tenant_id: tenant_a.id, source_article_id: article_a1.id, target_article_id: article_a2.id})
steps: Query for article_links belonging to tenant_a
expected_results: Query returns empty list -- tenant_b cannot see tenant_a's article links

test_cases.name: Article link is immutable -- no updated_at field
preconditions: Loopctl.Knowledge.ArticleLink module is compiled and available
steps: Inspect the schema fields of ArticleLink

technical_notes: Schema MUST use `use Loopctl.Schema` which provides `@primary_key {:id, :binary_id, autogenerate: true}`, `@foreign_key_type :binary_id`, `@timestamps_opts [type: :utc_datetime_usec]`.
Override timestamps to only include inserted_at: `timestamps(updated_at: false)` in the schema block.
Use `tenant_field()` macro in the schema block to add the tenant_id field with correct FK and index.
Relationship type enum: Ecto.Enum with values [:relates_to, :derived_from, :contradicts, :supersedes].

Self-link validation in changeset: `validate_change(:target_article_id, fn :target_article_id, target_id -> if target_id == get_field(changeset, :source_article_id), do: [target_article_id: "cannot link an article to itself"], else: [] end)`.
Foreign keys should use `on_delete: :delete_all` so cascading article deletion cleans up links.
Migration file: `priv/repo/migrations/TIMESTAMP_create_article_links.exs`. Import `Loopctl.Repo.RlsHelpers` and call `enable_rls(:article_links)` after table creation.

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs in DataCase/ConnCase setup, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex, (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests.
Add build(:article_link, attrs) and fixture(:article_link, attrs) to test/support/fixtures.ex with auto-dependency resolution for tenant_id, source_article_id, and target_article_id.
